intro: Desmodium arechavaletae, also known as "Cuñapirú" or "Cuñapirá," is a plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) that is native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Desmodium arechavaletae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Desmodium arechavaletae is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a tincture, to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
